findings.evidence: `rg -n "derivative_hermite"` shows line 43 has `@[simp]` for `derivative_hermite_succ`, while lines 58-59 expose `derivative (hermite n) = n • hermite (n - 1)` without an attribute; the roadmap A1 entry requests the Hermite derivative API be simp-facing.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `derivative_hermite` or otherwise provide the same simp-facing normal form for arbitrary `n`.
findings.issue: `Polynomial.derivative_hermite` is the public arbitrary-index normal form for `derivative (hermite n)`, but it lacks `@[simp]`, so `simp` only handles syntactic successor indices via `derivative_hermite_succ`.

findings.evidence: Lines 67-68 expose `derivative^[k] (hermite n) = n.descFactorial k • hermite (n - k)` with no automation attribute, and the right-hand side removes the iterated derivative without introducing a loop.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `iterate_derivative_hermite` if it is the intended normal form, or add a simp-facing wrapper with the same left-hand side.
findings.issue: `Polynomial.iterate_derivative_hermite` is the public closed form for `derivative^[k] (hermite n)`, but it is not registered as a normal-form simp lemma, leaving the exposed iterated derivative API manual to use.

summary: The public Hermite derivative surface is still missing automation on the exposed characteristic derivative restatements, so downstream users cannot normalize the arbitrary-index and iterated forms with `simp`.
